description: Automated alert: 401 error rate exceeded 10% threshold on auth service
Customer support ticket opened: users being logged out mid-session
On-call engineer paged; initial hypothesis is load balancer sticky session drop
Automated alert: JWT signature mismatch on 12% of token validations since secret rotation
Root cause confirmed: JWT secret rotation at 09:15 did not retain old key in JWKS endpoint; old tokens rejected
Ticket opened: Login broken after maintenance - Invalid credentials for correct passwords
Previous JWT signing key added back to JWKS endpoint; new token validations succeeding
Hotfix v2.14.1 deployed: dual-key JWKS validation restored; ~3400 sessions invalidated requiring re-login
401 error rate dropping; monitoring for 30 minutes before marking resolved
INC-4019 resolved: error rate back to baseline; post-mortem scheduled; key rotation runbook to be updated
Stripe status page reports degraded performance on payments API; circuit breaker opened on gateway→payments edge
Customer ticket: payments service timing out; checkout hanging indefinitely
Customer email: cards charged but orders not placed; suspected race condition
On-call engineer engaged; confirmed Stripe degraded on Stripe status page; no infra action needed
Alert: high failed transaction volume; Redis cache miss spike noted (later confirmed unrelated)
Gateway circuit breaker opened for payments upstream after consecutive 503 responses (misconfigured threshold=1)
100% of API calls returning 502 Bad Gateway; nginx logs show upstream connection refused
CRITICAL: orders-db-primary Postgres data volume at 100% capacity; writes failing
Orders service returning HTTP 500 since 14:32; pods restarted twice; memory graphs elevated
Root cause confirmed: WAL archive on orders-db not pruned for 47 days due to misconfigured cron job; disk at 100%
Customer-facing 500 on checkout; suspected inventory-check microservice blocking thread pool
WAL archive pruning started; 180GB freed; EBS volume extension initiated (500GB)
Stripe resolved their incident at 12:51 UTC; payments circuit breaker reset automatically; payment queue draining
Orders-db-primary restarted successfully; orders service recovered; 500s resolved
Fixed misconfigured WAL archive cron job schedule; disk alerting thresholds set at 75% and 90%
Gateway circuit breaker reset after payments upstream recovery; 502s resolved
INC-4021 payments sub-incident resolved; Stripe fully recovered; no data loss
INC-4021 orders sub-incident resolved; disk usage stable; cron job fixed; post-mortem scheduled
Redis cluster OOM: used memory exceeded maxmemory limit; cache writes failing with OOM error
Auth service degraded: session writes to Redis failing; users being logged out; fallback to DB triggered
Root cause: Redis maxmemory-policy set to noeviction; cache filled completely under load spike
Changed eviction policy to allkeys-lru; flushed stale keys; memory pressure relieved
INC-3908 resolved: Redis operating normally; eviction policy permanently updated; memory alerting added
